Divinity/Celestial Prescence.md.title: Celestial Prescence
Divinity/Celestial Prescence.md.summary: Celestial Presences are astral phenomena tied to Outer God worship, appearing in the sky above devotional centers. The strongest display is above Hallia, Calaria's capital.
keyFacts: Each Outer God has a unique sign appearing in the sky above centers devoted to their worship.
The strength of the Celestial Presence scales with the intensity of prayers offered.
Areas with many Gods' influence produce especially spectacular night sky displays.
Hallia, the capital of Calaria, is said to have the most impressive Celestial Presence of anywhere.
The Iridescent Host's aurora brightens the sky above Hallia during the darkest nights.
The Iridescent Host's rainbow hue can outshine even the suns on days of particularly fervent reverence.

Divinity/Divine Raiment.md.title: Divine Raiment
Divinity/Divine Raiment.md.summary: Divine Raiment is a phenomenon where divine blessing overflows a mortal's vessel, typically manifesting as rays of light around the head.
keyFacts: Raiments usually manifest as rays of light surrounding one's head.
Highly blessed individuals can extend the effect further, such as coating the whole body as holy armor.
Aurelia VII can reportedly coat her whole body in the Host's light as holy armor.
A raiment's color and sheen are derived from the deity that granted the blessing.
Followers of the Iridescent Host have pearlescent, shimmering raiments.
Followers of the Judge of Ages have radiant golden raiments.
Raiments allow individual variation in color and form.
Observers can typically identify how blessed someone is, and by which deity, at a glance.

Divinity/Fiends/Sigzil Haight.md.title: Sigzil Haight
Divinity/Fiends/Sigzil Haight.md.summary: Sigzil Haight, the Shackled Prince, is a fiendish prince and child of the Judge of Ages whose demesne lies along the Infinite Horizon.
keyFacts: Known by the epithet 'the Shackled Prince'
Is a child of the Judge of Ages
Holds the rank of fiendish prince
His demesne is located somewhere far out along the Infinite Horizon

Divinity/Godly Interplay.md.title: Godly Interplay
Divinity/Godly Interplay.md.summary: An overview of cooperation and conflict between the Outer Gods' religious organizations, noting alliances are temporary and wars are fought in their names.
keyFacts: The Watcher and the Pulse have compatible teachings; their followers often cooperate, especially in the Belvedere.
The Heir and the Compelled share views on an inevitable future, with followers often agreeing on major details.
The Iridescent Host preaches dominance, making interfaith cooperation a 'necessary evil' for its followers.
The Heir and the Pulse have directly antithetical teachings, causing clashes between their devotees.
The Judge's followers frequently insert themselves into others' affairs, earning both gratitude and derision.
The true goals of the Outer Gods are described as inscrutable.
Any alliances between Outer Gods or their followers are considered only temporary.
Wars have been fought between nations on behalf of their Gods and are expected to continue.

Divinity/Hierophant.md.title: Hierophant
Divinity/Hierophant.md.summary: A Hierophant is one who has passed through the Firmament into the Godhome and met the Gods. Aurelia was the first, ushering in a new age for Færrin.
keyFacts: Magic arrived on Færrin when an unknown force pierced the Firmament, causing chaos and societal collapse.
Aurelia, a Calarian peasant, was the first to hear an Outer God (the Iridescent Host) and widened the crack in the Firm…
Aurelia's passage into the Godhome created a second sun and is marked as day one of Færrin's calendar.
Soothsayers use Harrow Decks featuring hierophant likenesses for fortune-telling; each card has an image and personalit…
IX The Lamp: Silfane, Provost of Belvedere — insight into the unknown.
XI Justice: Farrow Cross, High Arbiter of The Scale — truth revealed.
XVI The Tower: identity unknown; hierophant of the Heir of the Plague — implies upheaval or disaster.
XX The Æon: Aurelia VII, Pontifex of the Iridescent Church — portends awakening or reckoning.

Divinity/Inner Gods.md.title: Inner Gods
Divinity/Inner Gods.md.summary: Inner Gods are deities who exist within Færrin's firmament — whether summoned, ascended from mortality, or small enough to pass through from the Astra.
keyFacts: Outer Gods cannot pass through the firmament onto Færrin's surface.
Inner Gods either passed through the firmament or formed whole-cloth on the inside.
Some Inner Gods are sacred emissaries sent by Outer Gods as their eyes and ears.
Some Inner Gods are demigods — mortals elevated beyond their mortal coil by a source of power.
A small handful are full Gods from elsewhere in the Astra that could fit through the firmament.
Aurelia I was the first to pave the way to the Godhome.
Many on Færrin devote themselves to Inner Gods rather than Outer Gods.
Blessings from Inner Gods vary widely, and diverse raiment lights are not rare in crowds.

Divinity/Outer Gods/Eternal Pulse.md.title: Eternal Pulse
Divinity/Outer Gods/Eternal Pulse.md.summary: The Eternal Pulse is an Outer God patronizing doctors and necromancers, embodying the tension between preserving life and pursuing undeath.
keyFacts: Category: Outer God
Edicts: Heal wounds and treat sicknesses, pursue undeath
Anathema: Sacrifice your life, abandon the wounded or dying
Divine Ability: Strength or Constitution; Divine Font: harm or heal
Favored Weapon: morningstar; Divine Skill: Medicine
Domains: freedom, healing, nothingness, pain, soul, undeath
Cleric Spells: grim tendrils (1st), false vitality (2nd), mask of terror (7th)
No country openly devotes itself to the Pulse due to stigma around undeath, but followers are welcomed as guests

Divinity/Outer Gods/Heir of the Plague.m.title: Heir of the Plague
Divinity/Outer Gods/Heir of the Plague.m.summary: The Heir of the Plague is an Outer God tied to the cycle of life, death, and pestilence, with worshippers found across the world in small numbers.
keyFacts: Outer God; patron of no specific country but has scattered worshippers worldwide.
Edicts: abide by life's cycles, aid childbirth, destroy undead.
Anathema: pursuing immortality, creating undead, despoiling the earth, killing juvenile creatures.
Divine Raiment is deep red and fiery; Celestial Presence is a bleeding moon.
Favored weapon is the scythe; Divine Skill is Nature.
Domains: air, decay, disorientation, nature, plague, sun.
Divine Font: harm or heal; can choose holy or unholy sanctification.
Cleric Spells: 1st endure, 3rd wall of thorns, 5th blister.

Divinity/Outer Gods/Iridescent Host.md.title: Iridescent Host
Divinity/Outer Gods/Iridescent Host.md.summary: The Iridescent Host is an Outer God and patron deity of the Iridescent Church and the Thearchy of Calaria, favoring her people's prosperity and cultural legacy.
keyFacts: Outer God; patron of the Iridescent Church and the Thearchy of Calaria.
Edicts: establish cultural superiority, create great works.
Anathema: forgo credit for your accomplishments.
Divine Raiment is pearlescent and shimmering; Celestial Presence is an Aurora.
Favored weapon is the mace; Divine Skill is Diplomacy.
Divine Font: harm or heal; can choose holy or unholy sanctification.
Domains: confidence, cities, indulgence, passion, toil, wealth.
Cleric Spells: 1st soothe, 4th creation, 6th collective transposition.

Divinity/Outer Gods/Judge of Ages.md.title: Judge of Ages
Divinity/Outer Gods/Judge of Ages.md.summary: The Judge of Ages is an Outer God and patron of The Scale, acting as an intermediary between Outer Gods and championing impartial justice and world balance.
keyFacts: Patron deity of The Scale organization.
Edicts: perform just executions, study local laws, oppose corrupt or bloodthirsty officials.
Anathema: kill without thought, execute the innocent, mock the condemned, falsely incriminate another.
Divine Raiment is golden and radiant; Celestial Presence rings from horizon to horizon.
Favored weapon is the greataxe; Divine Skill is Society.
Divine Font: harm or heal; Divine Sanctification: can choose holy or unholy.
Domains: death, duty, might, swarm, truth, vigil.
Cleric Spells: sure strike (1st), paralyze (3rd), mountain resilience (4th).

Divinity/Outer Gods/The Compelled.md.title: The Compelled
Divinity/Outer Gods/The Compelled.md.summary: The Compelled is an Outer God, former patron deity of the Raelion Empire, teaching predestination, meritocracy, and self-perfection.
keyFacts: Outer God; was the patron deity of the Raelion Empire, now worshipped by Raelish refugees.
Teaches predestination: all have an ideal life, and followers must strive to reach it.
Emphasizes meritocratic hierarchies and respect for superiors.
Edicts: achieve your best self, work hard, and demand others do as well.
Anathema: disobey a superior, shirk your duties.
Divine Ability: Intelligence or Dexterity; Divine Skill: Arcana; Favored Weapon: whip.
Domains: fate, glyph, introspection, luck, magic, perfection.
Cleric Spells: message rune (1st), secret page (3rd), never mind (6th).

Divinity/Outer Gods/Watcher With 1,000 E.title: Watcher With 1,000 Eyes
Divinity/Outer Gods/Watcher With 1,000 E.summary: The Watcher With 1,000 Eyes is an Outer God and patron of Belvedere, teaching followers to chronicle existence and advance society toward an inevitable future.
keyFacts: Outer God and patron deity of Belvedere
Edicts: chronicle existence, uncover hidden mysteries, advance society
Anathema: destroy sources of knowledge, hinder forward technological progress
Divine Raiment is pure white and twinkling; Celestial Presence is Nebulae
Favored weapon is the glaive; Divine Skill is Occultism
Domains: change, cold, creation, darkness, knowledge, star
Cleric Spells: penumbral shroud (1st), stupefy (2nd), blanket of stars (6th)
Devotees choose Dexterity or Intelligence; Divine Font is harm or heal; can choose holy or unholy

Divinity/The Narrative/Aut.md.title: Aut
Divinity/The Narrative/Aut.md.summary: Aut is an unsettling humanoid entity who observes and subtly manipulates the world through proxies, residing in the Black and White.
keyFacts: Completely white skin with oversized hands and feet, gangly limbs, and a cylindrical torso
His head is a perfectly spherical, oversized sphere with no facial features except a giant toothy grin
He prefers to influence the world indirectly through his 'friends' rather than acting himself
The recent advent of the Voidsong has disrupted his ability to influence the world
He resides somewhere within the Black and White

Divinity/Unknown Entities/Green Father.m.title: Green Father
Divinity/Unknown Entities/Green Father.m.summary: The Green Father is a mysterious Inner God residing in the Verdant Expanse, believed to encourage agricultural cultivation of arable lands.
keyFacts: Category: Inner God
Resides within the Verdant Expanse
Encourages agricultural cultivation of arable lands
Edicts: Tend to the land, grow new and strange flora
Anathema: Tear down existing plantlife, disrupt the natural order
Divine Raiment: Green, floral
Celestial Presence: Occasional green flares

Divinity/Unknown Entities/The Zorbon.md.title: The Zorbon
Divinity/Unknown Entities/The Zorbon.md.summary: The Zorbon is a rumored mind-controlling entity with fungal qualities, whose influence is said to be repelled by tinfoil.
keyFacts: The Zorbon supposedly controls the minds of unsuspecting individuals.
Tinfoil is believed to repel the Zorbon's influence.
Followers fashion tinfoil clothing, including hats, to protect themselves.
The Zorbon has been described as having fungal qualities.
The Zorbon is believed to be somehow related to Benjamin Grayson, CEO of Amber Call.

Divinity/index.md.title: Divinity
Divinity/index.md.summary: Six Gods bless the world of Færrin but cannot physically enter due to a crack in the Firmament. They make their presence known through followers in Calaria and beyond.
keyFacts: Exactly six Gods are active in Færrin.
The Gods are referred to as 'Outer Gods'.
A crack in the Firmament prevents the Gods from physically entering the world.
The Gods have devout followers among the people of Calaria and beyond.
The Gods make their presence known despite being unable to enter the world.

Geography/Austrene/index.md.title: Austrene
Geography/Austrene/index.md.summary: Austrene is a high-altitude kingdom known for luxury alpine tourism and cutting-edge philosophical research conducted at the observatories of Belvedere.
keyFacts: Austrene features jagged peaks, glaciers, geothermic springs, and high-altitude observatories.
Affluent tourists visit for snow sports, hiking, and geothermic springs; locals have built palatial lodges.
Austrene has a reputation as a playground for the rich and powerful across Edan.
The observatories of Belvedere study the slip, where the firmament is thinnest.
Researchers at Belvedere are devoted to the Watcher With 1,000 Eyes and the Eternal Pulse.
Austrene is technically a kingdom with a king, but the royal family is more attraction than authority.
Real power is held by the Provost of Belvedere and a handful of resort-controlling oligarchs.
A Belvedere chat log references a hidden firmament thinning anomaly above outpost C12, escalated to the Provost.

Geography/Black and White/Pharos.md.title: Pharos
Geography/Black and White/Pharos.md.summary: Pharos is an infinitely tall concrete edifice on Færrin, visible everywhere but only to those already introduced to it.
keyFacts: Infinitely tall concrete building visible from anywhere on Færrin
Only perceivable by those who have already been introduced to it
Has an entrance at 13 Bright Street in Aurideon
Connects to disparate locations across the planet and other planes
Navigation through the building follows the ritualistic 'Rule of Three'

Geography/Black and White/index.md.title: Black and White
Geography/Black and White/index.md.summary: The Black and White is a separate world connected to Færrin via the Pharos.
keyFacts: The Black and White is a world distinct from Færrin.
It is connected to Færrin through a structure or phenomenon called the Pharos.

Geography/Brithwyn/index.md.title: Brithwyn
Geography/Brithwyn/index.md.summary: Brithwyn is an independent nation that broke away from Calaria ~40 years ago, distinguished by its strict limits on Orgs and a reinstated noble class.
keyFacts: Brithwyn declared independence from Calaria after the Calarian Prime Minister was assassinated, following a short but b…
Calaria's council recognized Brithwyn as independent rather than continue fighting.
Brithwyn limits how deeply Orgs can integrate into the country, unlike Calaria.
Brithwyn reinstated a nobility system to manage territory and public works, offering title and capital as incentives.
The Regent Administrator leads Brithwyn, with nobles handling day-to-day governance.
Worship of the Iridescent Host remains the predominant religion, same as in Calaria.
Chorath Engineering is a Brithwyn-based company producing vehicles like the 862 Aventine truck.
The Aventine features Palladium-Alloy plating, Rite-Drive Slide Suspension, and optional Folded Space™ storage.

Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Aurideon/index..title: Aurideon
Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Aurideon/index..summary: Aurideon is a sovereign city-state in Hallia, governed exclusively by the Iridescent Church and covered by a crystal dome that amplifies celestial aurorae.
keyFacts: Aurideon is the seat of power for the Iridescent Church.
It is one of two sovereign city-states within Hallia.
The Church governs it exclusively; the broader Calarian government has little say.
Entry is restricted for non-believers, with exceptions made for business.
The city is covered by a giant crystal dome.
The dome is structured to amplify the Iridescent Host's aurorae overhead.
Most convox of the Iridescent Church are headquartered in Aurideon.

Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Sableclutch/Sin.title: Sin and Tonic
Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Sableclutch/Sin.summary: The Sin and Tonic is a dingy bar in Sableclutch, owned by Illmari Vaino and serving as Iconoclasm's current headquarters.
keyFacts: Located in Sableclutch.
Owned and operated by Illmari Vaino.
Serves as the current headquarters of the organization Iconoclasm.
Interior is dingy and dark with tobacco incense censors hanging from the ceiling.
Main area features booths along the walls and a bar in the center.
Left exit leads to a workshop and garage.
Right exit leads to a stockroom and armory.
Back exit leads to bedrooms and an office.

Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Sableclutch/The.title: The Crowded Flea
Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Sableclutch/The.summary: The Crowded Flea is a riverside pub in Sableclutch, popular with dockworkers and serving as the main hideout of the Roundhat Gang.
keyFacts: Located on the edge of the Fousan River in Sableclutch.
Popular gathering spot for local dockworkers.
Serves as the main hideout of the Roundhat Gang.

Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Sableclutch/ind.title: Sableclutch
Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Sableclutch/ind.summary: Sableclutch is a poor southern district of Hallia, situated on the south bank of the Fousan River and dominated by dockworkers and warehouse employees.
keyFacts: Located at the southernmost point of Hallia, the capital city.
Sits along the south bank of the Fousan River.
Predominantly inhabited by dockworkers and warehouse employees.
Many goods entering the city begin their journey through Sableclutch.
Considered a poorer, overlooked neighborhood by the rest of the capital.
The organizations that manage river trade have their power centers elsewhere.

Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Teller's Run/in.title: Teller's Run
Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Teller's Run/in.summary: Teller's Run is a neighborhood located at the northernmost point of Hallia, a city in the Calaria region.
keyFacts: Teller's Run is situated at the northernmost point of Hallia.
It was originally planned to be part of a larger development (details cut off).

Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Widdershins/ind.title: Widdershins
Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Widdershins/ind.summary: Widdershins is a poor neighborhood in southern Hallia, situated on the north bank of the Fousan River.
keyFacts: Located in the southern part of the city of Hallia.
Sits along the north bank of the Fousan River.
Considered a poorer neighborhood.
Home to many service industry workers who cannot afford to live near their workplaces.

Geography/Calaria/Verdant Expanse.md.title: Verdant Expanse
Geography/Calaria/Verdant Expanse.md.summary: The Verdant Expanse is a vast, ancient forest spanning a thousand miles between Calaria and the Tenchwin Sea, filled with dangerous creatures.
keyFacts: Spans approximately a thousand miles wide
Located between Calaria and the Tenchwin Sea
Trees reach up to a mile tall, blocking sunlight from the forest floor
Strange creatures prowl its darkened depths
Also known as the Green Sea
Actively resists outside subjugation or conquest
Modern civilization has largely abandoned attempts to explore its deepest regions

Geography/Calaria/Wrenford.md.title: Wrenford
Geography/Calaria/Wrenford.md.summary: Wrenford is a small, half-developed city on the edge of the Verdant Expanse, founded by Calarian Orgs for logging that was abandoned after the forest resisted.
keyFacts: Built by multiple Orgs from Calaria to harvest timber from the Verdant Expanse.
The Verdant Expanse 'fought back,' making logging too expensive to continue.
The city never reached its intended potential but did not die out either.
Wrenford remains half-developed as a result of the failed logging venture.
Many residents follow the Green Father in addition to the Iridescent Host.

Geography/Calaria/index.md.title: Calaria
Geography/Calaria/index.md.summary: Calaria is the dominant superpower of Færrin, controlling trade, media, and military globally, governed jointly by the Iridescent Church and a corporate council.
keyFacts: Calaria dominates trade, media, music, and military power across the continent of Færrin.
Calarian culture is marked by intense national pride, described by outsiders as jingoism.
The economy is powered by the strongest industrial complex in the planet's history.
Most citizens work as cogs in large private corporations known as Orgs.
Calaria's rapid growth was driven by divine blessings, the Iridescent Church, and expansion into the Infinite Horizon.
Government is jointly run by the Iridescent Church and a rotating council of powerful Orgs.
Each Org nominates ministers to the council, which internally elects a Prime Minister.
The Prime Minister and the Pontifex (head of the Iridescent Church) co-govern the country.

Geography/Fenrith/index.md.title: Fenrith
Geography/Fenrith/index.md.summary: Fenrith is a coastal nation whose inhabitants live in underwater glass dome-cities, dominating ocean trade due to their mastery of sea currents.
keyFacts: The Termean Veinlands covers 85%+ of Fenrith's territory: hot, soft-grounded tundra unsuitable for crops or buildings.
Most Fenrithi live in reinforced glass dome-cities built beneath the ocean surface along the western coast.
Fenrithi ships travel fully submerged, and citizens farm and ranch ocean-native animals for food.
Fenrith is a mercantile powerhouse; most large cargo companies are operated by or with the Fenrithi.
Fenrith is ruled by the Fisher-Kings: the four heads of the largest cargo companies.
There is no state religion; The Compelled has the largest following, but all faiths have a presence.
The Veinlands is home to fugitives, sovereign citizens, and homesteaders; traversed using modified automobiles.
A sample shipping manifest includes kelp flour, Istrian silk, Tormerén marble, weapons, and seerhorse corpses.

Geography/Færrin.md.title: Færrin
Geography/Færrin.md.summary: Færrin is a formerly magic-less planet near the galaxy's core, unique in the Astra for its mundane origins before the recent arrival of magic.
keyFacts: Færrin is located near the galaxy's core.
Until recently, Færrin had no magic, no gods, and no stars in its sky.
Magic is the one universal thread connecting all worlds in the Astra.
Færrin's people lived for hundreds of thousands of years believing they were alone in the universe.
Before magic arrived, the only sentient life on Færrin was human.
After magic was introduced, more than half of humans developed new traits.
Sentient magic beasts appeared across the planet following the influx of magical energy.

Geography/Istria/index.md.title: Istria
Geography/Istria/index.md.summary: Istria is a cursed, war-scarred land where a roaming corruption called the Stillness forces inhabitants to live in mobile caravan-cities called striders.
keyFacts: The Stillness is a living curse that corrupts anything remaining stationary too long, turning victims to crystal-cracke…
Istria was devastated by wars during the dark ages before Aurelia's enlightenment.
Inhabitants live in giant mobile caravan-cities called striders, constantly moving to survive.
Powerful artifacts from the Dark Ages remain untouched across the landscape.
High curse density causes magic to crystallize into a dense, valuable power source.
Istrians are renowned for wanderlust; one was the first to circumnavigate the globe.
The Istrian mobile spaceport is the closest any Færrin group has come to leaving the atmosphere.
Istria is a key participant in the Infinite Horizon exploration effort.

Geography/Lorandris/index.md.title: Lorandris
Geography/Lorandris/index.md.summary: Lorandris is a loose alliance of extra-continental enclaves on the Fūlwinter coast, infamous for piracy but indispensable to Edan for its unrivaled shipbuilding.
keyFacts: Lorandris is a loose alliance of enclaves settled by people removed from Edan proper, located on the Fūlwinter coast.
Its official economy is trade and bulk transport, but its largest economic driver is piracy.
Lorandrin pirates raid coastal towns and capture cargo ships at sea.
With airframes, pirates use massive 'skybreaker' ships to raid inland settlements before governments can respond.
Lorandrin ships are faster, more durable, higher-capacity, and some can turn invisible — no other country can match the…
Every country on Edan buys ships from Lorandris, making the nation politically untouchable despite its small size.
Mere Lorandrin citizenship is a felony in other countries, so Lorandrin travelers hide their origins.
Natives of Lorandris all work in building, sailing, or sinking ships.

Geography/Quiet Below/Nos Mortem.md.title: Nos Mortem
Geography/Quiet Below/Nos Mortem.md.summary: Nos Mortem is a Victorian-style manor located in a fane deep beneath Hallia, owned by Benjamin Grayson and staffed by undead.
keyFacts: Victorian-style manor situated in a fane deep below the city of Hallia.
Owned by Benjamin Grayson.
Staff consists of skeletons, ghosts, and ghouls.

Geography/Quiet Below/index.md.title: Quiet Below
Geography/Quiet Below/index.md.summary: The Quiet Below is a vast network of caverns deep underground, largely unexplored and home only to those hiding from society.
keyFacts: A series of caverns snaking miles below the continent's surface.
No great civilizations have ever lived there.
No unique resources exist there that couldn't be gathered closer to the surface.
Rarely visited due to lack of incentive.
Superstition holds that the Gods' blessings are harder to receive underground.
Dangerous monsters are believed to lurk in the depths.
Only inhabitants are those actively avoiding the public eye.

Geography/Rhædon/index.md.title: Rhædon
Geography/Rhædon/index.md.summary: Rhædon is an isolated nation on the continent of Edan, historically cut off by the Siksithys desert and Tenchwin cape, now emerging as a global player.
keyFacts: Rhædon is separated from the rest of Edan by the Siksithys desert (land) and the Tenchwin cape (sea).
Before airframes and easy flight, Rhædinan people rarely left their country due to dangerous travel.
Rhædon has been undercutting competitors in trade to break into continental markets.
A major setback occurred when Rhædon's staunchest ally was 'bombed out of existence.'
Historically, most of the population worked in agrarian fields due to forced self-sufficiency.
Outside organizations have moved manufacturing to Rhædon to capitalize on lower wages.
Young Rhædinans now migrate toward the rest of the continent for work.
Asaid Al-Taiiq gave an expansionist inauguration speech declaring Rhædon's rise on the world stage.

Geography/Tormeré/index.md.title: Tormeré
Geography/Tormeré/index.md.summary: Tormeré is a strikingly beautiful, democratically governed country in the Iridescent Belt, known for pink sands, paraíba gemstones, and agricultural exports.
keyFacts: Part of the 'Iridescent Belt' alongside Calaria and Brithwyn, where the Iridescent Host's presence is strongest.
National gemstone is the paraíba, a blue dust that sparkles across the ground.
Native cheiro flowers perfume the air; carrilhão trees chime in the wind.
Major exports include sweet fruit liqueurs, oils, and a mild hallucinogen called erva-teme.
Tormeré has a fully democratic government, described as a rarity on Edan.
The Tormerén government defers to the Iridescent Church on relevant matters.
Calaria acts as Tormeré's protector and staunch ally on the global stage.
Sculptor Paolo Barreto caused controversy with a nude depiction of the Outer Gods; the Iridescent Church censured him f…

Geography/Velthara/index.md.title: Velthara
Geography/Velthara/index.md.summary: Velthara is a mountainous, resource-focused nation whose people resisted modernization. Their cities grow vertically across the Infinite Horizon, spanning hundreds of worlds.
keyFacts: Velthara's population is predominantly miners and natural-resource workers.
Veltharans resisted electricity, radio, automobiles, and personal credit; newer generations are slowly adopting them.
Veltharan cities are 'strand-cities' with small footprints but span hundreds of worlds along the Horizon.
Public trolleys travel between worlds, ferrying hundreds of thousands of people daily.
The capital, Tor's Strand, starts in Star 612, ends in Void 87, and is still growing.
Velthara was historically family-led; individual worlds were controlled by a matriarch or patriarch.
Families have recently banded into clan-nations to compete globally; most people now belong to one of a few clans.
An entire section of Wil's Strand in Star 13 mysteriously disappeared; the Blain Clan denied responsibility.

Geography/index.md.title: Geography
Geography/index.md.summary: Færrin is a world currently in a cold-war era following Calaria's destruction of Raelion 40 years ago, replacing open warfare with espionage and political maneuvering.
keyFacts: Færrin is experiencing its longest period without overt conflict since before Aurelia I's enlightenment.
Calaria destroyed rival nation Raelion on the night of the Midnight Sun, 40 years ago.
The threat of total annihilation has prevented outright war between nations since.
Open warfare has been replaced by psychological warfare, propaganda, espionage, embargoes, and tech competitions.
Nine countries total participate in this political climate, including Calaria.
Alliances are described as tenuous and fickle, shifting over decades.
The primary continent is Edan, where Calaria and the other nations reside.
Other continents exist on Færrin but their peoples rarely travel to Edan.

Org/Alkahest Freight/People/Kadou Floch..title: Kadou Floch
Org/Alkahest Freight/People/Kadou Floch..summary: Kadou Floch is a half-human, half-elven member of Alkahest Freight, known for his fluid grace and alchemical work.
keyFacts: Wire-thin, sharp-featured figure of human and elven heritage
Moves with an uncanny fluid grace
Favors a dark green coat with synthetic silk
Wears a monocle over his right eye that constantly clicks through its lenses
Fingertips are permanently stained amber from his work
Member of Alkahest Freight

Org/Alkahest Freight/index.md.title: Alkahest Freight
Org/Alkahest Freight/index.md.summary: Alkahest Freight is a logistics company specializing in cross-horizon shipping, supplying many far-horizon colonies.
keyFacts: Alkahest Freight specializes in cross-horizon shipping.
Many far-horizon colonies have been founded using supplies brought by Alkahest Freight.

Org/Amber Call/People/Benjamin Grayson.m.title: Benjamin Grayson
Org/Amber Call/People/Benjamin Grayson.m.summary: Benjamin Grayson is the human CEO of Amber Call, Calaria's top health insurance provider, and a secret high-level necromancer and Pulse follower.
keyFacts: Appears approximately 30 years old, with stark white hair and gray, slate-colored eyes.
Bears a large raiment of the Eternal Pulse.
Current CEO of Amber Call, the foremost premium health insurance provider in Calaria.
Is a follower of the Eternal Pulse.
Secretly a high-level necromancer.
Currently resides in Nos Mortem.

Org/Amber Call/People/Mr. Whiskers.md.title: Mr. Whiskers
Org/Amber Call/People/Mr. Whiskers.md.summary: Mr. Whiskers was a black and white mottled catfolk street-dweller of Sableclutch, killed by Benjamin Grayson in a necromantic ritual.
keyFacts: Mr. Whiskers was a black and white mottled catfolk.
He lived on the streets of Sableclutch.
He was a member of the Anti-Zorbon Taskforce.
The Anti-Zorbon Taskforce met in the sewers beneath Widdershins.
He was slain by Benjamin Grayson.
His death was used in a necromantic ritual.

Org/Amber Call/People/Ossain Madrun.md.title: Ossain Madrun
Org/Amber Call/People/Ossain Madrun.md.summary: Ossain Madrun is a skeletal undead majordomo serving Nos Mortem, recognizable by his red glowing eyes and broken jaw.
keyFacts: Ossain is a skeleton standing 5'6" tall.
He has deep red glowing points of light for eyes.
His jaw is broken in three places.
He serves as the majordomo of Nos Mortem.

Org/Amber Call/People/Silven Grayson.md.title: Silven Grayson
Org/Amber Call/People/Silven Grayson.md.summary: Silven Grayson is a sharply-dressed human member of the Amber Call, notable for his white hair, gray eyes, and amber-and-black formal attire.
keyFacts: Human male in his mid-twenties
Has stark-white hair and cool slate-gray eyes (a family trait)
Wears immaculate amber-and-black formal wear
A subtle stylized pulse motif is stitched into the lining of his clothing
Member of the Amber Call

Org/Amber Call/index.md.title: Amber Call
Org/Amber Call/index.md.summary: Amber Call is a medical rescue and insurance company that provides care to plan holders in exchange for payment.
keyFacts: Amber Call is a medical rescue and insurance company.
Services are plan-based — customers must have an active plan to receive care.
Known for responding to even minor injuries (e.g., a papercut).
Always accompanies medical response with a bill.

Org/Belvedere.md.title: Belvedere
Org/Belvedere.md.summary: Belvedere is an ancient organization founded to chronicle the world, which evolved to include spies, scholars, aristocrats, and divine entities called Hearts.
keyFacts: Founded shortly after the Dark Ages ended, mandated by the Watcher With 1,000 Eyes to chronicle Færrin.
The first Provost established the original chancery in Austrene, atop the planet's highest mountains.
Originally a fact-recording outfit, Belvedere expanded due to pressure from rival Orgs seeking its secrets.
Political operatives (socialites and spies) were added to study people as well as natural phenomena.
Hearts are slivers of sentient divine energy gifted by the Outer Gods, used to process vast amounts of data.
Hearts became coordinators and leaders; many members now report to a Heart instead of a person.
Members can specialize as academics, archivists, aristocrats, or arcanists.
Arcanists work closely with the Hearts and their divine machinery.

Org/Candescent Legion.md.title: Candescent Legion
Org/Candescent Legion.md.summary: The Candescent Legion is a private army known for being cheap but low-quality, typically used as expendable fodder alongside smaller, better-trained forces.
keyFacts: Is a private army available for hire.
Notably cheaper than alternative military options.
Considered low quality — 'you get what you pay for'.
Primarily used as fodder to supplement smaller, better-trained fighting forces.

Org/Children of the Heir.md.title: Children of the Heir
Org/Children of the Heir.md.summary: The Children of the Heir are worshippers of the Heir of the Plague who act as her agents, working to restore the natural order against disruption.
keyFacts: Serve the Heir of the Plague, who abhors disruption of the natural circle of life.
Consider deforestation, draining marshlands, and cybernetic augmentation as breaks in the circle of life.
Members are called 'Children' and carry out the Heir's will in varied ways.
Some act as lobbyists and lawyers to protect nature through legal means.
Others take extreme action such as bombing construction sites or animating the earth.
All members tend to adopt an animalistic or floral motif to signal allegiance to the Heir.

Org/Chorath Engineering.md.title: Chorath Engineering
Org/Chorath Engineering.md.summary: Chorath Engineering is a Brithwyn-based automobile manufacturer producing vehicles for city driving, combat, and Infinite Horizon exploration.
keyFacts: Based in Brithwyn
Designs and builds automobiles
Produces vehicles suited for city driving, shootouts, and exploration
Serves customers venturing into the Infinite Horizon

Org/Concordia.md.title: Concordia
Org/Concordia.md.summary: Concordia is a major manufacturing organization in Calaria that pivoted from magical goods to modern appliances and machinery by using magic in their assembly lines.
keyFacts: Concordia operates in the continent of Færrin, specifically in Calaria.
They pivoted from scroll production to household appliances like dishwashers, freezers, and air conditioners.
Concordia employs skilled mages of all creeds to design and build their assembly lines.
Their assembly lines use magic directly: steel that twists itself into shape, self-assembling motors, etc.
They manufacture a wide range of goods: watches, televisions, construction machinery, and more.
Concordia capitalized on the volatility caused by rapid technological advancement in Færrin.
Before modern tech, long-distance communication used familiars and sending stones, not radios or phones.
The technological shift made many former magical industry giants obsolete, but Concordia adapted successfully.

Org/Garrick Textiles/People/Letov Obratz.title: Letov Obratz
Org/Garrick Textiles/People/Letov Obratz.summary: Letov Obratz is a tiefling member of Garrick Textiles, notable for his distinctive appearance and mood-reactive iridescent suit.
keyFacts: Purple-skinned tiefling with small, polished obsidian horns.
Wears a complex multi-layered suit of shifting, iridescent fabrics.
His suit changes texture with his mood.
Described as dapper in appearance.
Member of Garrick Textiles.

Org/Garrick Textiles/index.md.title: Garrick Textiles
Org/Garrick Textiles/index.md.summary: Garrick Textiles is a Concordia subsidiary that produces fabrics and clothing for the majority of Edan.
keyFacts: Garrick Textiles is a subsidiary of Concordia.
It produces fabrics and clothing.
Its products serve the majority of Edan.

Org/Ghosts of Raelion.md.title: Ghosts of Raelion
Org/Ghosts of Raelion.md.summary: The Ghosts of Raelion are a fractured organization of Raelish survivors united by the destruction of their homeland, split across three competing factions.
keyFacts: Originally a mercenary group from Raelion who were abroad when the Midnight Sun destroyed their country.
Founded by Donovan Hale, who swore vengeance for Raelion and all who perished.
Grew from a small merc group into a broader movement by recruiting Raelish survivors across the continent.
Has no formal hierarchy; members operate in independent 'cells' that coordinate only when necessary.
Hale's faction seeks to dismantle systems that caused Raelion's destruction, ranging from disarmament to retaliation.
Juno Bright, a former Raelic Church priest, leads a faction focused on reuniting disenfranchised Raelish people.
Calder Marran leads a third faction that wants to abandon the past and forge a new independent identity.
Cells typically align with one faction but mixed allegiances within a single cell are not uncommon.

Org/Hildebrandt Corporation/People/Paavo.title: Paavo Karhu
Org/Hildebrandt Corporation/People/Paavo.summary: Paavo Karhu is an elderly winterkin changeling member of the Hildebrandt Corporation, notable for his imposing appearance and refined dress.
keyFacts: Winterkin changeling with skin like cracked ice and a snow-white beard
Described as massive and elderly in build
Wears a heavy tailored velvet overcoat with diamond-buttoned lapels
Moves slowly with a silver-headed cane
Member of the Hildebrandt Corporation

Org/Hildebrandt Corporation/index.md.title: Hildebrandt Corporation
Org/Hildebrandt Corporation/index.md.summary: Hildebrandt Corporation is an airframe company pioneering the commercial flight industry, run by a fortune dragon named Hildebrandt.
keyFacts: Hildebrandt Corporation is an airframe manufacturing company.
The company is a pioneer in the commercial flight industry.
The organization is led by Hildebrandt, a fortune dragon.

Org/Hollowpact.md.title: Hollowpact
Org/Hollowpact.md.summary: Hollowpact is a mechanized infantry unit from Istria, specializing in rapid strike operations against enemy encampments.
keyFacts: Hollowpact is a mechanized infantry unit.
Originates from the region of Istria.
Specializes in fast hit-and-run raids on enemy encampments.
Designed to strike and withdraw before the enemy can mount a proper response.

Org/Iconoclasm/People/Anaïs Marchal.md.title: Anaïs Marchal
Org/Iconoclasm/People/Anaïs Marchal.md.summary: Anaïs Marchal is a reserved, magically-inclined human member of the Iconoclasm, and fraternal twin of Anouk Marchal.
keyFacts: Late 20s human male with red hair and dark brown eyes
Fraternal twin of Anouk Marchal
Wears sharp plain suits with wooden rings and bracelets
Bears a moderately sized raiment of the Iridescent Host
More magically-inclined than his twin sister
His workshop area is filled with magical tomes and alchemical reagents
More reserved in personality compared to his sister Anouk

Org/Iconoclasm/People/Anouk Marchal.md.title: Anouk Marchal
Org/Iconoclasm/People/Anouk Marchal.md.summary: Anouk Marchal is a human member of the Iconoclasm, a technologically-focused twin who works with electrical equipment in her workshop.
keyFacts: Late 20s human female with red hair and dark brown eyes
Fraternal twin of Anaïs Marchal
Wears practical workshop jumpsuits to protect against burns and stains
Has a moderately sized raiment of the Iridescent Host
Her workshop area is filled with multimeters, oscilloscopes, and electrical equipment
More technologically-inclined of the twins
More outgoing than her brother (twin sibling)

Org/Iconoclasm/People/Dura Oil-Drinker.m.title: Dura Oil-Drinker
Org/Iconoclasm/People/Dura Oil-Drinker.m.summary: Dura Oil-Drinker is an orcish mechanic and founding member of Iconoclasm, handling vehicle maintenance, outside contacts, and the group's finances.
keyFacts: Mid-40s orcish woman with dark green skin and curly black hair.
Has two tusks on her bottom jaw; one has been cut to a stump.
Wears mechanics overalls and dark welding goggles.
Bears a small raiment of the Iridescent Host.
Serves as Iconoclasm's resident gearhead and mechanic.
Maintains the company car and manages the group ledger.
Handles contacts with outside organizations on behalf of Iconoclasm.
Was the first person to join Iconoclasm when Illmari Vaino founded it.

Org/Iconoclasm/People/Elias Ramsey.md.title: Elias Ramsey
Org/Iconoclasm/People/Elias Ramsey.md.summary: Elias Ramsey is an elderly dwarven quartermaster for Iconoclasm, known for his curmudgeonly attitude and skill at locating lost items.
keyFacts: Elderly, dark-skinned dwarven male with wispy blonde-gray hair and a balding head.
Keeps a short-trimmed mustache and beard.
Wears tweed suits with elbow patches and reading glasses on a chain.
Bears a small raiment of the Iridescent Host.
Serves as quartermaster for Iconoclasm.
Known as the group curmudgeon — prefers doing things his own way.
Excels at locating items lost by other Iconoclasm members.

Org/Iconoclasm/People/Gin Soaked Rag.md.title: Gin Soaked Rag
Org/Iconoclasm/People/Gin Soaked Rag.md.summary: Gin Soaked Rag (Ginny) is a Heart entity resembling a dishrag in a suit who maintains the Sin and Tonic fane.
keyFacts: Appears as a 6-foot-tall dishrag wearing a three-piece suit
Floating suit arms end in gloves that move independently
Face is formed by the dishrag scrunching up; liquid beads at his edges when he moves
Holds the role of Heart at the Sin and Tonic fane
Friends with Illmari Vaino and the rest of Iconoclasm
Considered part of the Iconoclasm team despite being unable to leave the bar

Org/Iconoclasm/People/Illmari Vaino.md.title: Illmari Vaino
Org/Iconoclasm/People/Illmari Vaino.md.summary: Illmari Vaino is a half-elf founder of the mercenary group Iconoclasm and proprietor of the Sin and Tonic, drawn together by the Voidsong.
keyFacts: Late-60s half-elf male with black slicked-back hair and facial scars.
Wears a raiment of the Iridescent Host.
Current proprietor of the Sin and Tonic.
Founder of the mercenary group Iconoclasm.
Was one of the first to capitalize on hearing the Voidsong.
Collected like-minded people to investigate the Voidsong's source and meaning.

Org/Iconoclasm/index.md.title: Iconoclasm
Org/Iconoclasm/index.md.summary: Iconoclasm is a mercenary organization founded in Hallia that became the premier group investigating the mysterious Voidsong after an unusual concentration of its members heard the dreams.
keyFacts: Founded by Ilmari Vaino, who was among the first to hear the Voidsong.
Originally a small-time freelance mercenary group operating out of Hallia.
Has the highest concentration of Voidsong listeners on the continent.
Grew rapidly as listeners from other Orgs joined seeking answers.
Other Orgs have donated staffing and funding to Iconoclasm as a show of goodwill.
Orgs also fund Iconoclasm to prevent rivals from gaining an advantage.
Player characters are recently joined members, either loaned by an Org or independent.

Org/Iridescent Church/Convox/Magical Cri.title: Detective Ellis Brooks
Org/Iridescent Church/Convox/Magical Cri.summary: Detective Ellis Brooks is a seasoned middle-aged human detective in the Magical Crimes division, known for his pragmatic instincts and leadership role.
keyFacts: Middle-aged human male with graying hair, typically hidden under a fedora
Wears an open brown peacoat over a disheveled white button-down shirt
Carries a pistol in a chest holster worn over his shirt
Bears a moderately sized raiment of the Iridescent Host
No-nonsense personality who trusts his gut over dismissing the strange
Prefers fieldwork but is too valuable as a coordinator to justify it
Member of the Iridescent Church's Convox, Magical Crimes division

Org/Iridescent Church/Convox/Magical Cri.title: Magical Crimes
Org/Iridescent Church/Convox/Magical Cri.summary: The Magical Crimes Convox is an Iridescent Church department that investigates unexplained supernatural events harming the Host's people.
keyFacts: Department of the Iridescent Church focused on solving weird/unexplained negative magical events.
Previously investigated a mysterious curse originating from the Verdant Expanse.
Previously investigated an everlasting rain caused by excessive augury.
Headquartered at 33 Rook's Crossing in the Aurideon.

Org/Iridescent Church/People/Walter Stok.title: Walter Stoke
Org/Iridescent Church/People/Walter Stok.summary: Walter Stoke is an elderly, blind human priest of the Iridescent Church, known for his vestments woven from prismatic glass fibers.
keyFacts: Walter Stoke is elderly and blind.
He is a human priest.
He is a member of the Iridescent Church.
His vestments are heavy and layered, woven from translucent, prismatic glass fibers.
His vestments catch and scatter light.

Org/Iridescent Church/index.md.title: Iridescent Church
Org/Iridescent Church/index.md.summary: The Iridescent Church is the most powerful organization on Færrin, founded by Hierophant Aurelia I to spread the Iridescent Host's teachings.
keyFacts: Founded by the first Hierophant, Aurelia I, originally as a small collective of supporters.
Led by the Pontifex, currently Aurelia VII, who is said to be a reincarnation gifted by the Host.
Most influential in the 'Iridescent Belt': Calaria, Brithwyn, and Tormeré.
In Calaria, the Church is part of government and worship of the Host is the official state religion.
In Tormeré, the Church holds an advisory role and the people defer to its leadership.
Brithwyn has a Dissident Church that disputes whether Aurelia VII is a true reincarnation.
The Church is divided into semi-independent sub-organizations called convox, each with distinct responsibilities.
Notable convox include the Suric convox (priest training) and the Forma convox (manages churches of Hallia).

Org/Laity.md.title: Laity
Org/Laity.md.summary: Laity is a military contractor widely understood to be a covert arm of the Iridescent Church, used when deniability is needed.
keyFacts: Officially classified as an independent military contractor.
Commonly known to be an extension of the Iridescent Church.
Used by the Iridescent Church to operate in areas requiring deniability.

Org/Ministry of Cultural Progress/People.title: Giannos Hasikos

wikilinks.raw: [[Org/Ministry of Cultural Progress/index|Ministry of Cultural Progress]]

Org/Ministry of Cultural Progress/People.summary: Giannos Hasikos is a nondescript half-elf member of the Ministry of Cultural Progress.
keyFacts: Giannos Hasikos is a half-elf.
He has average, nondescript features and a mundane bearing.
He is a member of the Ministry of Cultural Progress.

Org/Ministry of Cultural Progress/index..title: Ministry of Cultural Progress
Org/Ministry of Cultural Progress/index..summary: MoCuP is a government organization holding broadcast rights across Calaria and Tormeré, managing radio channels, music stations, dramas, and screamsheet editorials.
keyFacts: Owns exclusive rights to broadcast on the radio spectrum in Calaria and Tormeré.
Manages broadcasting channels and curates music stations.
Produces radio dramas and screamsheet editorials.
Listening to MoCuP stations is described as a popular pastime across the country.

Org/NecroLog/People/Gaspar Caetano.md.title: Gaspar Caetano
Org/NecroLog/People/Gaspar Caetano.md.summary: Gaspar Caetano is a dhampir member of NecroLog, recognizable by his gaunt appearance and formaldehyde-scented specimen pouches.
keyFacts: Gaspar Caetano is a dhampir.
He is gaunt and pale with sharp fangs and dark circles under his eyes.
He wears a heavy leather coat lined with specimen pouches.
His specimen pouches smell of formaldehyde.
He is a member of the organization NecroLog.

Org/NecroLog/index.md.title: NecroLog
Org/NecroLog/index.md.summary: NecroLog is a medical technology manufacturer with strong ties to the Pale Lantern Society.
keyFacts: NecroLog is a medical technology manufacturer.
NecroLog has heavy ties to the Pale Lantern Society.

Org/Opaline Reserve.md.title: Opaline Reserve
Org/Opaline Reserve.md.summary: The Opaline Reserve is a racketeering operation disguised as a brewery in Hallia's Widdershins district.
keyFacts: Operates as a brewery in the Widdershins district of Hallia.
Suspected of conducting illegal augury within city limits.
Under suspicion by the Magical Crimes convox of the Iridescent Church.

Org/Pale Lantern Society/People/Manus Su.title: Manus Sudrenus
Org/Pale Lantern Society/People/Manus Su.summary: Manus Sudrenus is a high-elven aristocrat and member of the Pale Lantern Society, known for his silver hair and dark silk robes.
keyFacts: High-elven aristocrat with a sleek appearance
Has silver hair tied back with a silken ribbon
Wears flowing dark silk robes
Member of the Pale Lantern Society

Org/Pale Lantern Society/index.md.title: Pale Lantern Society
Org/Pale Lantern Society/index.md.summary: The Pale Lantern Society is Færrin's oldest active organization, a once-secret cult pursuing immortality through necromancy, now endorsed by the Outer God known as the Pulse.
keyFacts: Predates the Gods and the Iridescent Church, originating in the Dark Ages.
Originally a cabal of warlords and aristocrats practicing necromancy and memetics.
Operated clandestinely due to widespread opposition to raising the dead.
Gained public legitimacy when the Pulse declared them her worldly presence after the firmament cracked.
Now operates hospitals, research centers, and graveyards across the continent.
Famous for their Columbariums, where wealthy patrons' preserved corpses are displayed as art.
Columbarium bodies await the Society's perfection of necromancy to be truly resurrected.
Their ranks include both necromancers and healers, seen as closely related disciplines.

Org/Prime Meridian/People/Tas Millett.md.title: Tas Millett
Org/Prime Meridian/People/Tas Millett.md.summary: Tas Millett is a hobgoblin member of Prime Meridian, known for his regal bearing and navigator-themed attire.
keyFacts: Tall hobgoblin with slate-gray skin and sharp, chiseled features
Wears a meticulously pressed naval coat with gold-trimmed cartography lines
Carries an antique brass astrolabe at his belt
Member of the organization Prime Meridian

Org/Prime Meridian/index.md.title: Prime Meridian
Org/Prime Meridian/index.md.summary: Prime Meridian is a powerful infrastructure organization that controls roads and rail across Færrin, and famously staged a bloodless coup to dissolve Calaria's monarchy.
keyFacts: Founded by Forster Cain as a small transportation company in the era of horse-pulled trams.
Built the first rail system in Calaria through a deal with the then-king.
Grew into an infrastructure giant; all roads and trains in Calaria were built and maintained by PM.
Staged a bloodless coup in a joint announcement with the Iridescent Church.
The Calarian king was informed of his dissolution at the same time as the public.
The king stepped down after losing the Church's support and having no means of escape.
Cain and the Iridescent Church founded the Council that now rules Calaria.
Prime Meridian still builds roads and operates trains connecting most of Færrin after Cain's death.

Org/Protectorate/People/Tamara McKay.md.title: Tamara McKay
Org/Protectorate/People/Tamara McKay.md.summary: Tamara McKay is a high-ranking elven member of the Protectorate, known for her masked appearance and elegant dueling scars.
keyFacts: Female elf, typically wears high-ranking security armor with her face masked.
Her movements are described as fluid and relaxed, communicating expression despite the mask.
When unmasked, she has two distinct, elegant dueling scars on her jaw.
She is a member of the Protectorate organization.

Org/Protectorate/People/Yao Addington.md.title: Yao Addington
Org/Protectorate/People/Yao Addington.md.summary: Yao Addington is a male gnome member of the Protectorate, clad in heavy oversized tactical plating with a stylized visor hiding his face.
keyFacts: Male gnome
Wears heavy, oversized tactical plating that clanks with every step
Face hidden behind an intimidating stylized visor
Member of the Protectorate

Org/Protectorate/index.md.title: Protectorate
Org/Protectorate/index.md.summary: Protectorate is a Brithwyn-based private mercenary army, the sole outside organization permitted to operate in Brithwyn, known for elite soldiers called slayers.
keyFacts: Protectorate is the only outside organization permitted to operate in Brithwyn after its split from Calaria.
It is a private army-for-hire, originally available to anyone seeking to destabilize governments or wage wars.
Before the Brithwyn-Calaria split, Protectorate was the premier mercenary company in the region.
While the Calarian army focused on crusades, Protectorate served the highest bidder.
Protectorate soldiers helped Brithwyn break free from Calarian rule.
Protectorate now primarily serves as peacekeepers within Brithwyn, but still hires out for conflicts.
Their elite soldiers, called slayers, are trained to enter a powerful 'battletrance'.
Stories of entranced slayers singlehandedly destroying rival encampments are considered only slightly exaggerated.

Org/Radiant Arms/People/Ichel.md.title: Ichel
Org/Radiant Arms/People/Ichel.md.summary: Ichel is an android member of Radiant Arms, distinguished by porcelain-like skin, golden circuitry lines, and armor-plated corporate attire.
keyFacts: Ichel is an android.
She has smooth, porcelain-like synthetic skin.
Faint golden circuitry lines trace her throat and temples.
She wears a sleek armor-plated corporate dress.
She is a member of the organization Radiant Arms.

Org/Radiant Arms/index.md.title: Radiant Arms
Org/Radiant Arms/index.md.summary: Radiant Arms is a firearm manufacturer based in Calaria, specializing in high-caliber enchanted weapons.
keyFacts: Based in Calaria
Specializes in high-caliber, enchanted firearms
Motto: "Just a tap'll do ya!"

Org/Roundhat Gang/People/Jasper Glover.m.title: Jasper Glover
Org/Roundhat Gang/People/Jasper Glover.m.summary: Jasper Glover is a low-level henchman of the Roundhat Gang who was spared by the Party and sent back to his gang's hideout.
keyFacts: Low-level henchman in the Roundhat Gang
Was spared by the Party rather than killed or captured
Was allowed to return to the Roundhat Gang's hideout after being spared

Org/Roundhat Gang/People/Tywelwyn Leathe.title: Tywelwyn Leatherhide
Org/Roundhat Gang/People/Tywelwyn Leathe.summary: Tywelwyn Leatherhide is the founder and leader of the Roundhat Gang, a protection racket in Sableclutch, and a magically enhanced deerfolk.
keyFacts: Deerfolk standing 6'11" with dappled brown fur and tall, branching antlers.
His muscles are unnaturally large, suggesting magical or chemical enhancement.
His skin runs hot enough that his sweat sizzles on contact.
Founder and leader of the Roundhat Gang, a protection racket.
The Roundhat Gang operates in the city of Sableclutch.
Was rumored to have gone crazy and hear voices.
Has struck a deal with someone in the organization Pyrelight.
Formerly unable to wield magic; now pilots a simulacrum to act on his behalf.

Org/Roundhat Gang/index.md.title: Roundhat Gang
Org/Roundhat Gang/index.md.summary: The Roundhat Gang is a protection racket operating in Sableclutch, run by Tywelwyn Leatherhide out of The Crowded Flea.
keyFacts: The Roundhat Gang is a protection racket based in Sableclutch.
It is run by Tywelwyn Leatherhide.
The gang operates out of a location called The Crowded Flea.
The gang has recently established some kind of partnership with Pyrelight.

Org/RunGunners/People/Ceres.md.title: Ceres
Org/RunGunners/People/Ceres.md.summary: Ceres is a fetchling member of the RunGunners, recognizable by her grayscale appearance and twin customized hand cannons.
keyFacts: Fetchling with monochromatic grayscale skin and light-absorbing hair
Athletic and sharp-eyed in appearance
Wears an oil-stained duster over formal wear
Carries a pair of heavily customized heavy-frame hand cannons
Member of the RunGunners organization

Org/RunGunners/index.md.title: RunGunners
Org/RunGunners/index.md.summary: The RunGunners are an armored transport organization based in Fenrith, offering armed convoy services with live scrying and automatic tagging of attackers.
keyFacts: Operate as armored transport convoys for hire, moving people or goods safely.
Offer a live scry of transports during delivery as part of their guarantee.
Automatically attempt to magically tag anyone who attacks their convoys.
Based in the Termean Veinlands region of Fenrith.
Their vehicles are custom-built to handle soft, dry sand as well as paved roads.
Their corporate town, Throttlehold, is home to engineers, mechanics, and armed guards.

Org/Sable and Suns/People/Lark Crowe.md.title: Lark Crowe
Org/Sable and Suns/People/Lark Crowe.md.summary: Lark Crowe is a scarred tengu member of Sable and Suns, recognizable by his glossy black feathers and a vertical scar splitting his beak.
keyFacts: Lark Crowe is a tengu with glossy black feathers.
He has a distinctive scar that cracks his beak vertically in half.
He wears leather vests and rugged denim.
His style is described as common among old scouts from Austrene.
He is a member of the organization Sable and Suns.

Org/Sable and Suns/index.md.title: Sable and Suns
Org/Sable and Suns/index.md.summary: Sable and Suns is a military contractor supplying weapons, vehicles, and training consultants to multiple factions across Edan.
keyFacts: S&S is technically unaffiliated with any country in Edan, openly playing all sides.
They supply the Calarian military as one of its major contractors.
Protectorate, the Pirate-Kings of Lorandris, and Orgs from Rhædon also hold contracts with S&S.
Unlike other contractors, S&S sends consultants to teach clients how to use purchased weapons and vehicles.
S&S firearms and blades are high quality but expensive.
S&S-made weaponry in the hands of bandits or mercenaries signals wealthy backing.

Org/Sentinel Dynamics.md.title: Sentinel Dynamics
Org/Sentinel Dynamics.md.summary: Sentinel Dynamics is a private security organization offering bodyguard and guard services, considered a status symbol among the wealthy.
keyFacts: Provides personal bodyguard services for VIPs
Offers stationary guard services (e.g. art galleries)
Having a Sentinel as a bodyguard is considered a status symbol

Org/Solari Sub-surface/People/Salvado Ta.title: Salvado Tarragona
Org/Solari Sub-surface/People/Salvado Ta.summary: Salvado Tarragona is a scarred, stocky member of Solari Sub-surface, known for his underground career and heavy drinking.
keyFacts: Short, stocky, and densely muscled with leathery, scarred skin
His bloated eyes suggest a lifetime of heavy alcohol use
Wears a half-formal, half-flight-suit ensemble with brass buckles
Member of Solari Sub-surface

Org/Solari Sub-surface/index.md.title: Solari Sub-surface
Org/Solari Sub-surface/index.md.summary: Solari Sub-surface is a small shipping company that fills market gaps left by larger competitors.
keyFacts: Small shipping company operating in a niche market
Positions itself to fill gaps left by larger players like Prime Meridian and Alkahest Freight

Org/Ternion Heavy Industries/People/Jeth.title: Jethro Twill
Org/Ternion Heavy Industries/People/Jeth.summary: Jethro Twill is a human member of Ternion Heavy Industries, recognizable by his mechanical left arm, engineer's uniform, and weathered appearance.
keyFacts: Towering, broad-shouldered human male
Always wears a stiff engineer's uniform rather than formal attire
Has a matte-gray mechanical left arm that whirs faintly
Weathered face with an iron-gray beard
Carries a permanent smell of ozone and machine oil
Member of Ternion Heavy Industries

Org/Ternion Heavy Industries/index.md.title: Ternion Heavy Industries
Org/Ternion Heavy Industries/index.md.summary: Ternion Heavy Industries is an organization specializing in cargo airframes, known for multi-slip sliders used in uninhabited-world transport.
keyFacts: Major competitor to the Hildebrandt Corporation
Specializes in manufacturing airframes for transporting cargo
Known for multi-slip sliders that drop into uninhabited worlds for traffic-free transport

Org/The Harlequins/People/The Master of.title: The Master of Ceremonies
Org/The Harlequins/People/The Master of.summary: The Master of Ceremonies is the masked elven leader of The Harlequins, recognizable by his horned pale mask and blue-and-gold harlequin-patterned coat.
keyFacts: Tall, slender elf with a high-collared coat in a blue-and-gold diamond harlequin pattern.
Wears a pale, unblinking mask with small elegant horns, concealing his face.
Leads the organization known as The Harlequins.

Org/The Harlequins/index.md.title: The Harlequins
Org/The Harlequins/index.md.summary: The Harlequins are an organization responsible for building the Strider and organizing the pilgrimage.
keyFacts: Built the Strider
Organizers of the pilgrimage

Org/The Scale/People/Cassian Drake.md.title: Cassian Drake
Org/The Scale/People/Cassian Drake.md.summary: Cassian Drake is a heavily tattooed human member of The Scale, recognizable by his broken nose, shaved head, tactical greaves, and long-barreled rifle.
keyFacts: Burly, heavily tattooed human male
Has a broken nose and shaved head
Wears tactical greaves
Carries a long-barreled rifle
Member of the organization known as The Scale

Org/The Scale/People/Lance Vesper.md.title: Lance Vesper
Org/The Scale/People/Lance Vesper.md.summary: Lance Vesper is a sickly, lithe human member of The Scale, recognizable by his pale skin, rheumy eyes, and dark leather coat with hidden pockets.
keyFacts: Lithe and sickly in build with pale skin and rheumy eyes.
Wears a high-collared dark leather coat lined with hidden pockets.
Member of the organization known as The Scale.

Org/The Scale/People/Ralph Bishop.md.title: Ralph Bishop
Org/The Scale/People/Ralph Bishop.md.summary: Ralph Bishop is a scarred, golden-scaled lizardfolk member of The Scale, known for wearing a tailored silk waistcoat.
keyFacts: Heavily scarred with golden scales
Wears a tailored but slightly too-tight silk waistcoat
Member of The Scale

Org/The Scale/index.md.title: The Scale
Org/The Scale/index.md.summary: The Scale is an extranational organization serving the Judge of Ages, enforcing order, contracts, and balance across Færrin from their Stronghold in the Opaline Mountains.
keyFacts: Headquartered in a citadel called the Stronghold, located in the Opaline Mountains on a leyline conjunction.
Serves as the Judge of Ages' instrument of order and balance across Færrin.
Operates as an extranational governance body, independent of any nation.
Four ranked groups (rarest to most common): Timekeepers, Overseers, Arbiters, Pactbrokers.
Pactbrokers witness contract signings for a fee; magic monitors signees for compliance.
Breaking a magically-witnessed contract brings the full force of the Scale against the violator.
Arbiters track down those who have escaped justice and deliver punishment.
Overseers prevent attempts to seal the Gods away again, guarding against a return to the Dark Ages.

Org/Verdant Holdings/People/Cross Trevil.title: Cross Trevils
Org/Verdant Holdings/People/Cross Trevil.summary: Cross Trevils is an oread dwarf member of Verdant Holdings, notable for his rock-like skin, moss-lined jaw, and suit of living organic fiber.
keyFacts: Cross Trevils is an oread dwarf with rock-like skin.
Moss grows along his jawline and soil is permanently under his nails.
He wears a formal suit woven from living organic fiber.
He is a member of Verdant Holdings.

Org/Verdant Holdings/index.md.title: Verdant Holdings
Org/Verdant Holdings/index.md.summary: Verdant Holdings is an agricultural organization focused on high-density, high-yield crops to meet growing population demands.
keyFacts: Specializes in high-density, high-yield crop production
Operates as an agricultural powerhouse
Aims to keep pace with a rapidly growing population

Org/index.md.title: Org
Org/index.md.summary: On Færrin, multinational organizations called Orgs fill the power vacuum left by governments overwhelmed by rapid prosperity granted by the Gods.
keyFacts: On Færrin, Orgs handle safety and order instead of governments.
Divine blessings caused societies to grow faster than their power structures could manage.
Orgs rose by capitalizing on the resulting gaps in power.
The concept of the Org was founded by Forster Cain.
Forster Cain grew up poor in Sableclutch, a city in the Hallia region of Calaria.
As a child, Cain observed urban dysfunction: overcrowded streets, food shortages, idle noble estates.
Cain sketched imaginary city plans in the dust atop a neighborhood church roof.
Cain also founded an organization called Prime Meridian.

Org/Ætherion Limited/People/Braxton Hall.title: Braxton Hall
Org/Ætherion Limited/People/Braxton Hall.summary: Braxton Hall is a sylph member of Ætherion Limited, recognizable by drifting translucent hair and a brass containment harness.
keyFacts: Braxton Hall is a sylph.
Has wispy, translucent hair that drifts as if in a phantom breeze.
Wears a velvet doublet over a brass containment harness.
The brass containment harness is described as humming.
Is a member of Ætherion Limited.

Org/Ætherion Limited/index.md.title: Ætherion Limited
Org/Ætherion Limited/index.md.summary: Ætherion Limited is a continent-wide energy company that operates power plants supplying electricity to consumers of electric appliances.
keyFacts: Ætherion Limited operates power plants across the continent.
They are the primary electricity supplier for electric appliances in the setting.

Phenomena/Harmony/Voidsong.md.title: Voidsong
Phenomena/Harmony/Voidsong.md.summary: The Voidsong is a mysterious, world-spanning phenomenon — a haunting melody heard in dreams that lingers in waking life, dividing society between believers, fearers, and seekers.
keyFacts: The Voidsong belongs to the Divine and Occult traditions.
It manifests as a haunting, unrecallable melody first experienced in vivid dreams.
The dream features twin suns, a grand unplaceable city, and an unnamed, faceless figure who sings.
The song is described as a funeral dirge or a call to action.
The Voidsong has been reported by many people, surfacing in radio broadcasts, screamsheet editorials, and whispered con…
Society is divided over its meaning: some call it revelation, others a trap, others fear it is extraterrestrial.
Those marked by the Voidsong are driven to seek answers, leading them to Hallia and Iconoclasm.
It is described as wanting something — implying intent or agency behind the phenomenon.

Phenomena/Harmony/index.md.title: Harmony
Phenomena/Harmony/index.md.summary: Harmony is the form of magic on Færrin, ranging from subtle background forces to agentive powers that connect and empower chosen Listeners.
keyFacts: Some harmonies are soft and subtle, unfelt in daily life.
Other harmonies have agency and will themselves to be known by a chosen few.
Agentive harmonies connect their Listeners together.
Harmonies can impart knowledge or power on those who accept them.
The origin of a harmony is unknown.

Phenomena/Hearts.md.title: Hearts
Phenomena/Hearts.md.summary: Hearts are beings created by infusing a divine spark into an idea. They have physical forms, live in magical spaces called fanes, and can think and feel.
keyFacts: A heart is created by combining a divine spark with an idea, resulting in a tangible being.
Hearts choose a physical body shape once, but cannot change it afterward.
Hearts live in a fane — a space smaller on the outside, where they have great control; they cannot leave without explod…
Fanes can range from mousehole-sized to city-sized; size is based on magical power, which can be increased.
Hearts can alter the appearance of their fane freely, but forcing them to change it may provoke retaliation.
Hearts communicate with each other via a magical network called a skein; outsiders can join via a magic item called a t…
Hearts can be projected outside their fane temporarily when someone with their totem summons them.
Each major organization has a distinct heart naming convention (e.g., Pale Lantern Society uses poetic names; Protector…

Phenomena/Infinite Horizon.md.title: Infinite Horizon
Phenomena/Infinite Horizon.md.summary: The Infinite Horizon is a phenomenon making Færrin one of an infinite chain of parallel worlds, connected by ephemeral links traversable with simple magic devices called slides.
keyFacts: Færrin exists as an infinite chain of parallel versions of itself, with connections forming between adjacent worlds.
The two directions of travel are called 'voidward' and 'starward,' with Færrin prime as the origin point.
A magic device called a 'slide' is the standard tool for slipping between adjacent worlds; some can do so without one.
Adjacent worlds near Færrin prime were found empty, as if uninhabited versions of the planet.
The first sentient life was discovered at Star 46,656 — a sprite that had itself traveled from further starward.
Exploration is blocked in both directions by 'walls': travelers to Star 1,679,616 and Void 1,953,125 never returned.
Resource and land scarcity are non-issues since the same mines and fields exist across infinite worlds.
Faster traversal methods include random 'chutes,' powerful teleportation magic, and vehicles called Sliders.

Rules/Listener Dedication.md.title: Listener Dedication
Rules/Listener Dedication.md.summary: A character dedication representing a soul shaped by a Harmony's song. Listeners are bound to an enigmatic force connecting them to others.
keyFacts: All characters start with the Listener Dedication in addition to other creation options.
Listeners have heard a Harmony sing across the Infinite Horizon, shaping their soul.
Public reactions to Listeners include skepticism, disbelief, reverence, fear, and indifference.
Hearing a Harmony attunes the Listener to it, granting that Harmony's trait.
A character can only be attuned to one Harmony at a time.
If offered a second Harmony attunement, the character must choose only one to keep.
Spells and effects with Harmony traits are more powerful against creatures attuned to a different Harmony.
When using a choral ability, a character with a Harmony trait adds that trait to the ability.

Rules/Resonance Spells.md.title: Resonance Spells
Rules/Resonance Spells.md.summary: Resonance spells are a special spell type tied to Harmony attunement, cast using a dedicated Resonance pool rather than standard spell slots.
keyFacts: Resonance spells are gained only through special class features or feats, not chosen from a spell list.
They are cast using a Resonance pool, which is separate from normal spell slots.
Resonance spells auto-heighten to half your level rounded up, like cantrips.
Casting a resonance spell costs 1 Resonance; resonance cantrips cost nothing but require a pool.
Your Resonance pool maximum equals the number of resonance spells you know, or 3, whichever is lower.
Resonance cantrips do not count toward the pool's maximum size.
You replenish all Resonance during daily preparations; Refocus lets you regain 1 Resonance per use.
Resonance spells share the tradition of their granting Harmony; the Voidsong is both Divine and Occult.

Timeline.md.summary: A chronological timeline of major world events in Færrin, from prehistoric mundanity through 863ag when the Party joins Iconoclasm.

0ag: The Iridescent Host instructs Hierophant Aurelia to widen the crack, ending the Dark Ages and enabling divine magi…

index.md.title: Heart of Hearts
index.md.summary: A recovered log from the CIC Rule Of Three, capturing a three-part internal dialogue about witnessing a divine event and seizing forbidden knowledge.
keyFacts: The log is labeled signal sequence file №00-000-02 from the CIC Rule Of Three.
The log is loopbound, timestamped @ts000.01.01.000.00.018.
Three voices are present: L (left), C (center), and R (right).
L describes a 'first crack' splitting open with divine light pouring through.
R insists the knowledge is forbidden and only for 'the chosen, the hierophant, the blessed'.
C argues they are the 'selected/owned/tool' and were made by 'she' for this task.
C urges learning what can be learned while 'the path is still unclaimed'.
All three voices conclude in unison with 'glory'.
